name: MB Financial Bank
description_short: MB Financial Bank was a Chicago-based national bank offering traditional banking services until its 2019 merger with Fifth Third Bank. It operated as a full-service institution serving Illinois.
description_long:
MB Financial Bank, National Association was originally established as The Mid-City National Bank of Chicago on May 6, 1933. Over its 86-year history, the bank grew through aggressive acquisition strategy, particularly during the 2008-2010 financial crisis when it participated in multiple government-assisted mergers. The institution maintained its headquarters in Chicago, Illinois, with its final main office located at 800 West Madison Street.

As a national bank, MB Financial Bank offered traditional banking services including checking and savings accounts, credit products, and trust services through its network of branches across the Illinois metropolitan area. The bank's service area expanded significantly through acquisitions of regional financial institutions, allowing it to serve communities throughout Chicago and surrounding suburbs including Oak Brook, Elmhurst, Morton Grove, Lincolnwood, and South Holland.

MB Financial Bank distinguished itself through its role as an acquirer during the 2008-2009 financial crisis, participating in six government-assisted mergers that helped stabilize the regional banking market. The bank demonstrated stability and acquisition capacity when many institutions were struggling, absorbing failed and troubled institutions including Corus Bank, Heritage Community Bank, and InBank.

It is important to note that MB Financial Bank no longer operates as an independent entity. On May 4, 2019, the institution merged and became part of Fifth Third Bank, a major regional bank headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio. Customers seeking banking services previously offered through MB Financial Bank should contact Fifth Third Bank, which assumed all operations and accounts.
pros: Established institution with 86-year operating history dating to 1933
Significant regional presence across Illinois with multiple branch locations
Demonstrated acquisition and integration capability, absorbing 15+ institutions
Participated in stabilizing regional banking during 2008-2010 financial crisis
Maintained national bank charter and FDIC insurance protections
Expanded service area through strategic acquisitions including trust services institutions
cons: No longer operates independently—ceased operations as separate entity in May 2019
Historical data only; cannot open new accounts or access MB Financial Bank services directly
Customers must now work with Fifth Third Bank for any banking relationships
Limited operational history available for post-2010 period before merger
best_for: Former MB Financial Bank customers seeking to understand their account transition to Fifth Third Bank
Researchers studying regional bank consolidation and crisis-era banking during 2008-2010
Illinois residents interested in local banking history and regional financial institution evolution
diagnosis: MB Financial Bank is a historical case study rather than an active financial services option—the institution merged into Fifth Third Bank in 2019 and no longer operates independently. Current and former customers should contact Fifth Third Bank directly for banking services, account information, or account transitions.
